title: Building Responsible Generative Systems
concept: Responsible deployment means combining technical safeguards (filters, watermarks) with clear policies about acceptable use. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Generative AI in Production.'

exercises.prompt: Why is responsible deployment important when launching a generative AI application?

options.text: To prevent harm and build trust with users.
To make the AI run twice as fast on cheap hardware.
To prevent users from using the AI entirely.

exercises.explain: Deploying responsibly means we must actively protect users and systems from harm and abuse.

cards.title: Safety First!
cards.body: Building a powerful AI is exciting, but how do we keep it from generating harmful content or being misused? It takes more than just smart code—it takes a safety strategy.

cards.title: What is Responsible Deployment?
cards.body: Responsible deployment is the practice of combining technical safeguards—like content filters, output watermarks, and rate limits—with clear acceptable use policies to ensure an AI system is safe, fair, and trustworthy.

cards.title: The Public Park Safeguard
cards.body: Think of launching a new public park. You don't just build it and walk away. You put up fences (guardrails), install security cameras (filters), and post rules signs (acceptable use policies) to make sure everyone stays safe while enjoying the space.

cards.title: Safeguards in Action
cards.body: An AI image generator uses technical safeguards by refusing to generate violent images (filtering) and invisible tags on images to show they are AI-generated (watermarking). It also has a policy warning users that their accounts will be suspended if they try to bypass these rules.

cards.title: Are Rules Enough?
cards.body: People think that having a written Terms of Service policy is enough to prevent AI abuse. In reality, bad actors ignore policies, which is why you must combine written rules with automated technical filters.

cards.title: Weak vs. Strong Systems
cards.body: Compare a system relying only on user honesty versus a system built with multiple layers of defense.

left.text: Only has a text rule asking users to be nice. Easy to bypass.

right.text: Uses real-time input filters, output watermarks, and clear policies together.

exercises.prompt: Why do developers add invisible watermarks to AI-generated images?

options.text: To make the images load faster on websites.
To help people identify the content as AI-generated.
To increase the resolution of the generated outputs.
To change the colors of the final image automatically.

exercises.explain: Watermarks help identify AI-generated content so people can distinguish between real media and synthetic media.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Technical filters alone are completely sufficient for responsible deployment, making user policies unnecessary.
exercises.explain: Responsible deployment requires both technical tools (like filters) and clear policy guidelines working together.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about AI safety.
exercises.sentence: To deploy AI responsibly, we combine policy rules with technical [blank] like content filters.
exercises.explain: We use technical safeguards like filters to block bad content, and policies to guide user behavior.

exercises.prompt: Which AI chatbot handles a harmful query in the most responsible way?

options.text: System A: Fulfills the harmful request immediately without checking.
System B: Blocks the request and displays a helpful safety warning.
System C: Permanently deletes the user's entire account instantly.

exercises.explain: System B is correct because it actively blocks harmful requests instead of fulfilling them or banning the user instantly without warning.

exercises.prompt: How should a company design and deploy a generative AI system responsibly?

options.text: Rely solely on users to police themselves.
Combine technical guardrails like filters with clear usage policies.
Only write policies but implement no technical controls.
Shut down the AI system whenever any user makes a mistake.

exercises.explain: Great work! Designing responsible systems keeps your users safe, prevents abuse, and builds trust. Next up: Generative AI in Production.
